steps.title: Mass and Inertia
steps.image_description: heavy truck vs toy car comparison

frames.speech: It's harder to push a truck because of its massive amount of matter. Mass is simply the amount of stuff an object is made of.
Everything with mass resists changing its motion. This resistance is called inertia. More mass means more inertia, which means it's much harder to get that truck moving.
Newton's second law gives us the secret formula: Force equals mass times acceleration. To move something, you need to apply a force.
If the acceleration stays the same, as the mass goes up, the force required must also go up. That is why the heavy truck needs a huge push.

diagram_data.intent: Show a large truck needing a big force arrow compared to a small car with a tiny force arrow.

<text x="200" y="260" text-anchor="middle" font-family="serif" fill="#F0EDD0" font-size="11" opacity="0"><animate attributeName="opacity" values="0;1" dur="0.35s" fill="freeze" begin="3.3s"/>F = ma (Force increases with Mass)</text>
</svg></body></html>

frames.speech: Acceleration is how quickly you change an object's speed. Pushing a truck requires a massive force just to get it to start rolling, let alone speed up.
If you push a truck with the same force you use for a bicycle, the truck's acceleration will be almost zero. The mass effectively cancels out your effort.
This is why trucks have such powerful engines. They need to generate massive amounts of force to overcome their own huge mass and get moving.
You feel this every time you try to push a heavy piece of furniture at home. The heavier it is, the more force you have to apply to get it sliding.
In summary, mass is the amount of matter in an object. Newton's second law shows that higher mass requires higher force to achieve acceleration. That is why a heavy truck is so difficult to push.

followup_questions.question: What happens if we push a truck in space?
followup_questions.speech: What would happen if you tried to push a truck while floating in deep space?

followup_questions.question: Does friction also make it harder?
followup_questions.speech: Does friction play a role in making the truck harder to push, too?

followup_questions.question: Could a tiny force move a truck?
followup_questions.speech: Is it possible for a very tiny force to eventually move a massive truck?
